countries.name: Kenya
highlights: Strongest digital skills bootcamp density in East Africa
Nairobi hub drives entry-level tech hiring

countries.name: Nigeria
highlights: Largest youth population on the index — scale opportunity and pressure
Lagos & Abuja drive disproportionate entry-level tech demand

countries.name: South Africa
highlights: Mature policy frameworks but youth unemployment remains structurally high
Strong corporate graduate programs in finance and telecom

countries.name: Ghana
highlights: Stable democratic governance supports youth policy continuity
Accra emerging as a West African talent hub

countries.name: Rwanda
highlights: Highest pilot score — strong institutional coordination on youth skills
Kigali Innovation City anchors digital talent pipeline

countries.name: Egypt
highlights: Large graduate output with mismatch to private-sector demand
Cairo tech corridor growing but concentrated

countries.name: Ethiopia
highlights: Second-largest youth cohort — industrial parks expanding entry-level manufacturing
Digital infrastructure gap limits AI exposure at scale

countries.name: Morocco
highlights: Strong Francophone-Europe talent bridge via outsourcing sector
Casablanca & Rabat lead on graduate placement programs

countries.name: Senegal
highlights: Dakar digital ecosystem growing via francophone tech networks
Emerging focus on youth entrepreneurship through DER/FJ

countries.name: Tanzania
highlights: Dar es Salaam hub expanding but rural youth connectivity lags
Policy momentum on vocational reform under National Development Vision

countries.name: Uganda
highlights: Kampala tech ecosystem growing via mobile-money and fintech talent
Youth population growth outpaces formal job creation

countries.name: Côte d'Ivoire
highlights: Abidjan anchors francophone West Africa outsourcing growth
Post-conflict recovery driving vocational reform investment

countries.name: Tunisia
highlights: Strong tertiary education tradition but graduate unemployment persists
Tunis and Sfax tech corridors expanding offshore services

countries.name: Mauritius
highlights: Highest connectivity and skills infrastructure in the pilot cohort
Small population but strong conversion to offshore and financial services roles

countries.name: Botswana
highlights: Stable governance supports consistent youth policy implementation
Diamond economy diversification creating new entry-level sectors

countries.name: Cameroon
highlights: Bilingual economy offers unique regional talent bridge
Douala and Yaoundé tech scenes emerging but concentrated

countries.name: Algeria
highlights: Large graduate output with public-sector hiring dependency
Youth unemployment a persistent policy priority

countries.name: Angola
highlights: Oil-dependent economy limits diversified entry-level job creation
Luanda digital sector growing from low base

countries.name: Mozambique
highlights: Maputo corridor shows early tech and services growth
Connectivity and rural-urban divide constrain youth conversion at scale

countries.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
highlights: Largest youth cohort in Central Africa — conversion at scale is the challenge
Kinshasa fintech and mobile ecosystem offers early digital entry points

pillars.name: Education-to-Economy Pipeline
pillars.description: How effectively schooling, TVET, and higher education connect graduates to productive work.

pillars.name: Entry-Level Work & AI Exposure
pillars.description: Availability of first jobs, internships, and early-career roles that expose youth to digital and AI tools.

pillars.name: Institutional GenZ Readiness
pillars.description: Whether employers, public sector, and educators are structured to absorb and develop Gen Z talent.

pillars.name: Digital & AI Skills Infrastructure
pillars.description: Breadth of coding bootcamps, digital literacy programs, connectivity, and AI learning pathways.

pillars.name: Policy & Economic Participation
pillars.description: Youth employment policy, entrepreneurship support, and inclusion of young people in economic planning.

pillars.name: Cross-Border Talent Flow
pillars.description: Regional mobility, remote-work access, and pathways for African youth to compete in continental and global markets.
